original_description: Systematic debugging methodology. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT REPRODUCTION FIRST. Use when: debugging, bug won't reproduce, error investigation, "no funciona", "falla", "error". Trigger on: "bug", "debug", "no funciona", "error", "falla", "se rompe", "investigate".
best_description: Systematic debugging methodology. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT REPRODUCTION FIRST. Use when: debugging, bug won't reproduce, error investigation, "no funciona", "falla", "error". Trigger on: "bug", "debug", "no funciona", "error", "falla", "se rompe", "investigate".
final_description: Invoke when the user faces a broken or misbehaving system and doesn't know the root cause yet. Classic triggers: a service throws 500s or crashes, an endpoint is slow or times out, memory keeps growing, a test or feature that worked yesterday now fails, something works locally but breaks in production, a component doesn't render or behave as expected despite attempted fixes. Also invoke when the user explicitly says they've tried multiple things and it still doesn't work — that's the clearest signal. Provides structured root-cause methodology: reproduce first, trace backward from the symptom, hypothesize once, verify. Skip for: new feature requests, proactive refactoring, writing tests upfront, code review, or pure explanations.

history.description: Systematic debugging methodology. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT REPRODUCTION FIRST. Use when: debugging, bug won't reproduce, error investigation, "no funciona", "falla", "error". Trigger on: "bug", "debug", "no funciona", "error", "falla", "se rompe", "investigate".

history.description: Use when a user is investigating why something is broken, slow, crashing, or behaving unexpectedly — especially when they don't know the root cause yet. Apply to: production exceptions and stack traces, regressions (worked before, fails now), environment-specific failures (works locally, fails in prod), performance degradation (slowness, memory leaks, timeouts), components or endpoints not behaving as expected, and any situation where someone describes a symptom they can observe but can't explain. Distinctive signal: user is stuck trying to understand WHY something is happening, not just what to build next.
